title: Crepidiastrum denticulatum
intro: Crepidiastrum denticulatum is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Crepidiastrum denticulatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum denticulatum belongs to the family Tropaeolaceae, a small family of flowering plants. It is native to Asia and is also known as the 'denticulate crepidiastrum'. The classification of Crepidiastrum denticulatum is further divided into the genus Crepidiastrum and the species C. denticulatum. Within the family Tropaeolaceae, Crepidiastrum denticulatum is classified as a type of flowering plant with a unique set of characteristics. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and biodiversity within the plant kingdom.
uses: Crepidiastrum denticulatum is a plant species, however, information on its medicinal uses is limited.
